summary: F5 step 3-H synthetic upstream WS close BREAKTHROUGH at frame ordering layer. 'Close received after close' COMPLETELY GONE. WS close code transitioned from 1006 (abnormal) → 1000 (clean half-close). All wire-level fixes now working: keyword scan (#666 zlib inflate) + byte integrity (#669 atomic write) + frame ordering (#671 synthetic upstream close). However, DOM Korean warning still NOT rendered — JSON parses successfully (no SyntaxError, no 'Unknown command' log) but {command:'neo_error'} envelope is SILENTLY DROPPED by character.ai's onmessage dispatcher (handler ignores unrecognized commands without warning). Next axis: mimic legitimate server turn response envelope (add_turn / turn_state / similar) so existing turn renderer picks it up as assistant reply bubble.

close_received_after_close.diagnosis_confirmed: 47차 5-C sub-agent code review HP-A correct: server timeout → close-back was the root cause. Synthetic upstream close prevents this.

key_findings.1_frame_ordering_layer_solved: 47차 sub-agent diagnosis CONFIRMED. Synthetic upstream WS close (opcode 0x88, code 1000 to _sproxy) successfully prevents the server-timeout-close-back cycle. WS close transition 1006→1000 is direct evidence.
key_findings.2_three_layer_stack_complete: Cumulative wire-level fixes ALL WORKING: (#666) zlib inflate at keyword scan + (#669) atomic write for byte integrity + (#671) synthetic upstream close for frame ordering. Engine inject mechanism is now wire-correct.
key_findings.3_envelope_routing_remaining: Last layer: command schema. {command:'neo_error'} is silently dropped by character.ai's onmessage dispatcher (no 'Unknown command' log — dispatcher ignores unknown commands without warning). Need to mimic legitimate server turn response shape.
key_findings.4_mission_protection_unbroken_streak: Mission state from #666 → #667 → #668 → #669 → #670 → #671 has been HELD across all 6 iterations. Engine block layer is rock-solid; only UI delivery iterations changing.

recommendation.primary_diagnosis: Wire-level inject mechanism is COMPLETE. Next axis is purely envelope schema — find the command shape that character.ai's existing turn renderer recognizes.
next_steps: Engine team: try envelope shapes that mimic legitimate server turn responses. Likely candidates: {command:'add_turn', turn:{...}}, {command:'update_turn', turn:{...}, is_final_chunk:true}, {command:'turn_state', state:'completed', turn:{...}}
Inspect normal character.ai server traffic to capture exact turn response envelope schema (HAR capture during a non-PII chat)
Most promising: wrap warning text inside a fake 'assistant turn' so existing chat bubble rendering picks it up — UI handles it as if Adam McLeod said it
Continue current build in production — mission protection is fully working, only UX delivery needs schema work
Apply same lesson to copilot when copilot reaches this stage (frame ordering may also be broken there)
recommendation.mission_critical: No regression. Mission protection state from #666 still HELD across 6 iterations.
